site.title: Epistola · Document Intelligence Platform
site.description: Epistola helps operations teams author, govern, and deliver complex documents across every channel.
header.logo.subtitle: Documents
hero.description: Epistola only does one job: turn structured data into perfect PDFs. Model templates, validate JSON Schema inputs, and promote render-ready versions across environments without dragging workflows or AI "intelligence" along for the ride.
heroStats.0.label: Template versions per variant
heroStats.1.label: Render latency
heroStats.2.label: Role & environment isolation
trustedBy.logo.0.label: Company A
trustedBy.logo.1.label: Company B
trustedBy.logo.2.label: Company C
trustedBy.logo.3.label: Company D
video.title: From template to PDF in minutes.
howItWorks.title: A single path from template to PDF.
howItWorks.step.0.title: Model + author
howItWorks.step.0.body: Define input variables per template, manage variants, and drag blocks in the visual editor with live PDF preview.
howItWorks.step.1.title: Promote + govern
howItWorks.step.1.body: Freeze versions, assign them to staging/production environments, and gate releases behind reviewer approvals per tenant.
howItWorks.step.2.title: Render + deliver
howItWorks.step.2.body: Call the REST API or batch jobs, render PDFs in milliseconds, then push artifacts via storage adapters or webhooks.
whyUs.title: A single-purpose document generator you can trust.
whyUs.description: Keep your processes simple: orchestrate elsewhere, render here, and never worry about PDF fidelity again.
whyUs.reason.0.title: Open-source first
whyUs.reason.0.copy: Inspect every line of code, contribute renderers, and self-host on your own infrastructure.
whyUs.reason.1.title: Common Ground native
whyUs.reason.1.copy: Epistola speaks ZGW APIs and aligns with Dutch municipal standards out of the box.
whyUs.reason.2.title: Integrates anywhere
whyUs.reason.2.copy: Expose a simple HTTP API so workflow engines like GZAC or Valtimo stay in control.
whyUs.reason.3.title: Quality output
whyUs.reason.3.copy: PDF/A, PDF/X, web-ready bundles, and template testing pipelines keep every document consistent.
deploy.title: Keep orchestration elsewhere. Let Epistola handle the PDFs.
deploy.description: Self-host with Docker, run on Kubernetes, or consume the managed service. Either way, you stay in control of data, security, and release cadence.
everything.monoTitle: / Everything you need to render PDFs
everything.title: Epistola does templates and rendering — nothing else.
everything.description: Keep workflows in the products that orchestrate your cases while Epistola focuses on perfect output.
everything.card.0.title: Template lifecycle
everything.card.0.body: Variants, immutable versions, and draft/publish/archived states per tenant with audit trails and schema migrations.
everything.card.1.title: Visual editor
everything.card.1.body: Drag-and-drop blocks with conditional logic, loops, live PDF preview, and inline expression chips.
everything.card.2.title: Expression engine
everything.card.2.body: Use flexible expressions to calculate totals, format values, and apply conditional logic. Define input variables per template.
everything.card.3.title: Direct PDF renderer
everything.card.3.body: Render PDF/A and PDF/X outputs directly — no HTML conversion step. Handles 10,000+ documents per minute on simple hardware.
everything.card.4.title: Multi-tenant governance
everything.card.4.body: Role-based access, environment promotions (staging/production), and per-tenant default themes keep teams isolated.
everything.card.5.title: API + jobs
everything.card.5.body: OpenAPI 3.1 REST endpoints for single or batch renders with adaptive job sizing, webhooks, and storage adapters.
builtFor.title: Drop into ZGW, Valtimo, or bespoke stacks without touching workflows.
builtFor.description: Epistola exposes a narrow surface: JSON in, PDF/DOCX/HTML out. Multi-tenant auth via Keycloak, Helm charts, and health probes make it feel like any other platform service in your cluster.
builtFor.step.0.title: Submit payload
builtFor.step.0.description: Orchestrator sends signed JSON + tenant slug and registers a render batch.
builtFor.step.0.card.title: Payload intake · Besluit
builtFor.step.0.stage.0.label: Orchestrator
builtFor.step.0.stage.0.detail: Emit signed JSON + tenant slug; webhooks register batch IDs.
builtFor.step.0.stage.1.label: Validation
builtFor.step.0.stage.1.detail: Verify schema version, tenant access, and required fields.
builtFor.step.0.stage.2.label: Queue
builtFor.step.0.stage.2.detail: Assign job ID and enqueue render on the tenant pool.
builtFor.step.1.title: Render job
builtFor.step.1.description: Epistola resolves schema, selects template version, and renders output.
builtFor.step.1.card.title: Render job · Besluit
builtFor.step.1.stage.0.label: Template
builtFor.step.1.stage.0.detail: Resolve schema, pick active version, and lock template assets.
builtFor.step.1.stage.1.label: Renderer
builtFor.step.1.stage.1.detail: Render with audit logging and checksum hashing.
builtFor.step.1.stage.2.label: Artifacts
builtFor.step.1.stage.2.detail: Persist PDF/DOCX/HTML outputs and register storage refs.
builtFor.step.2.title: Deliver outputs
builtFor.step.2.description: Webhooks and storage callbacks provide URLs, metadata, and audit refs.
builtFor.step.2.card.title: Delivery · Besluit
builtFor.step.2.stage.0.label: Callbacks
builtFor.step.2.stage.0.detail: Send webhook with URLs, metadata, and audit references.
builtFor.step.2.stage.1.label: Storage
builtFor.step.2.stage.1.detail: Write outputs to tenant storage adapters and retain logs.
builtFor.step.2.stage.2.label: Archive
builtFor.step.2.stage.2.detail: Seal job metadata and surface history to orchestrators.
docops.title: Editor → template service → renderer: a single document generation lane.
docops.description: Epistola Suite is unapologetically about generation, not analytics or AI insights. Authors design templates, developers automate the render API, and platform teams run the Helm stack — all centered on getting compliant PDFs out the door.
docops.lane.0.title: Business authors
docops.lane.0.body: Drag/drop blocks, manage schema examples, and preview PDFs with inline expression evaluation.
docops.lane.0.bullet.0: Block library: text, tables, columns, clauses, headers, footers
docops.lane.1.title: Developers
docops.lane.1.body: Version templates in Git, run Testcontainers suites, and invoke the OpenAPI-render API from orchestrators.
docops.lane.1.bullet.0: API commands for template + theme automation
docops.lane.1.bullet.1: Adaptive batch jobs with job states and metrics
docops.lane.2.title: Platform ops
docops.lane.2.body: Deploy Helm charts with Keycloak/OIDC, monitor renders, and promote versions across environments.
architectureFlow.description: Epistola generates documents on demand. Click through the steps or let the animation play to see how data flows between systems.
architectureFlow.step.0.label: Caseworker starts process
architectureFlow.step.0.description: A caseworker initiates a case process in the zaakafhandelsysteem — for example handling an objection ("bezwaar").
architectureFlow.step.1.label: Collect case data
architectureFlow.step.1.description: The zaakafhandelsysteem gathers case data from the ZRC, ZTC, and other configured data sources.
architectureFlow.step.2.label: Request document generation
architectureFlow.step.2.description: Plugin sends a generation request to Epistola with the resolved template and all variable data.
architectureFlow.step.3.label: Render and store document
architectureFlow.step.3.description: Epistola renders the template with the provided data and stores the resulting PDF in temporary storage.
architectureFlow.step.4.label: Publish completion event
architectureFlow.step.4.description: Epistola publishes a notification via the Open Notificaties API to signal the document is ready for pickup.
architectureFlow.step.5.label: EpistolaDocumentGenerated
architectureFlow.step.5.description: Open Notificaties delivers the event callback to the zaakafhandelsysteem — the document is ready for pickup.
architectureFlow.step.6.label: Collect document
architectureFlow.step.6.description: The zaakafhandelsysteem uses the resource URL from the event to download the generated PDF from temporary storage.
architectureFlow.step.7.label: Register in Documenten API
architectureFlow.step.7.description: The zaakafhandelsysteem stores the PDF in the Documenten API (DRC), linking it to the original case for archival.
architectureFlow.step.8.label: Document available
architectureFlow.step.8.description: The caseworker can now view and download the generated document from within the zaakafhandelsysteem.
integrations.title: Wire Epistola in through the APIs you already own.
integrations.description: Common Ground support ships out of the box today; other orchestrators are delivered via custom adapters while we expand the integration catalog.
integrations.card.0.title: Common Ground ready
integrations.card.0.description: Built-in alignment with Common Ground APIs keeps municipalities compliant without reinventing orchestration.
integrations.card.1.title: Open REST API
integrations.card.1.description: Every template, clause, and delivery endpoint is controllable via our documented REST interface, ready for any stack.
integrations.card.2.title: Custom adapters
integrations.card.2.description: We co-design integrations for the orchestrators on your roadmap—no prebuilt connectors yet, but expert services on call.
integrations.roadmap.title: Integration roadmap
integrations.roadmap.description: We're actively validating packaged connectors for leading municipal orchestrators. Until those land, teams can move forward using REST endpoints, event hooks, and service engagements tailored to their environment.
integrators.title: Leave orchestration where it already lives.
integrators.description: Epistola exposes a guarded render surface that fits into Common Ground and bespoke platforms without forcing workflow rewrites.
integrators.highlight.0: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 validation before render
integrators.highlight.1: Immutable template versions per environment and tenant
integrators.highlight.2: OpenAPI 3.1 endpoints + adaptive batch job webhooks
integrators.renderWindow.label: render window (iText)
process.title: From payload mapping to delivery in three steps.
process.step.0.title: Map your data
process.step.0.description: Align your zaak, besluit, or custom payloads with template variables.
process.step.1.title: Link orchestrator
process.step.1.description: Call Epistola over HTTPS or message queue, send JSON, and await the PDF response.
process.step.2.title: Deliver documents
process.step.2.description: Push finished files into your DMS, send to citizens, or attach back to the case system.

testimonials.title: Trusted with high-stakes documents.
testimonials.0.quote: Epistola is the simplest part of our case platform. We send JSON, we get flawless PDF/A output back. That focus is refreshing.
testimonials.0.name: Customer Name
testimonials.1.quote: Common Ground compliance was non‑negotiable. Epistola spoke the same language from day one.
testimonials.1.name: Customer Name
testimonials.2.quote: We keep workflows in our orchestrator and let Epistola handle rendering. Separation of concerns, just like it should be.
testimonials.2.name: Customer Name
faq.title: Everything you need to know
faq.description: Answers about launch timelines, integrations, compliance, and support.
faq.0.question: How fast can we launch?
faq.0.answer: Timelines mainly depend on how quickly your infrastructure lands. Epistola ships everything you need on day one, so once access is provisioned we can start templating immediately. Our optional AI-assisted authoring trims the time it takes to shape bespoke templates.
faq.1.question: Can Epistola connect to our legacy systems?
faq.1.answer: We provide first-class integration with Common Ground and expose a REST API that any other system can call. Need something custom? We scope and deliver dedicated adapters on request.
faq.2.question: How is compliance handled?
faq.2.answer: Every implementation is tracked against a client-specific compliance model inside a bespoke register. All Epistola operations are logged so auditors can retrace every change and delivery.
faq.3.question: What support is included?
faq.3.answer: Scale and Enterprise plans include dedicated delivery managers, 24/7 support, and quarterly governance reviews.

features.hero.title: Epistola renders PDFs. Your products handle everything else.
features.hero.description: Templates, data bindings, and a rendering engine that respects Common Ground standards — all open source.
features.title: The Epistola Suite in practice
features.description: Straight from the reference implementation: how templates evolve, feedback gets routed, and assets stay governed.
features.section.0.title: Templates, variants, versions, environments
features.section.0.body.0: Each tenant owns template slugs with unlimited variants tagged by geography, brand, or audience.
features.section.0.body.1: Variants carry immutable version numbers with a draft → published → archived lifecycle and audit history.
features.section.0.body.2: Environments (staging, production, custom) activate specific versions so you can test or roll back safely.
features.section.0.body.3: Variant resolver inspects request attributes (e.g., language=nl) and picks the closest match automatically, falling back to the default when nothing fits.
features.section.1.title: Data schemas and executable examples
features.section.1.body.0: Every template ships with a JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 data contract validated pre-render.
features.section.1.body.1: Named example payloads live beside the schema for instant previewing and regression fixtures.
features.section.1.body.2: Schema migrations keep draft versions in sync when fields change between releases.
features.section.2.title: Built-in reviews with screenshots
features.section.2.body.0: Floating feedback button opens a modal that captures URL, user context, and console logs.
features.section.2.body.1: Drag in or paste screenshots—assets upload automatically and render in the feedback timeline.
features.section.2.body.2: Optional GitHub sync mirrors issues bi-directionally so engineers can triage where they work.
features.section.3.title: Tenant and variant theming
features.section.3.body.0: Reusable theme packages define typography, color tokens, and page settings (A4, Letter, margins).
features.section.3.body.1: Theme cascade respects tenant defaults, template overrides, then variant-specific tweaks for special cases.
features.section.3.body.2: Block-level presets make it easy to enforce branded tables, headers, and emphasis patterns.
features.section.4.title: Tenant asset manager
features.section.4.body.0: Upload PNG, JPG, SVG, or WebP up to 5 MB via drag-and-drop with automatic thumbnailing.
features.section.4.body.1: Assets live per tenant with search, delete, and direct references inside the template editor.
features.section.4.body.2: Raw endpoints serve optimized binaries to the renderer so logos stay crisp in every PDF.
templateSystem.title: How Epistola templates stay organized
templateSystem.description: Variants, immutable versions, JSON Schemas, and environment activations all live inside the suite so integrations stay simple.
templateSystem.schema.title: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
templateSystem.schema.examples.label: Examples
templateSystem.template.title: Public Decision Letter
templateSystem.template.description: Template slugs stay tenant-scoped. Variants inherit schema + theme but keep their own immutable version history.
templateSystem.template.resolverDescript: Generation requests can skip `variantId`. Provide attributes such as `language=nl` or `brand=business` and the resolver scores every variant, picking the closest match and falling back to the default if nothing satisfies required criteria.
templateSystem.template.resolver.title: Variant resolver
templateSystem.template.resolver.subtitl: Attribute intent → required filter → optional scoring → fallback safety net
Callers send attributes with required vs optional flags.
Any variant missing a required attribute drops out immediately.
Highest score wins; default variant stays as the safety net.
templateSystem.template.auditTrail.title: Audit trail
templateSystem.template.auditTrail.descr: Publishing locks the theme snapshot + schema hash so every request can be reproduced years later.
templateSystem.environments.title: Activate per stage
templateSystem.environments.description: Promote by switching the active version pointer; rollbacks are instant because older versions stay archived.
templateSystem.themes.title: Cascade across tenants → templates → variants
templateSystem.themes.cascade.0: Tenant default: Gemeente sans + amber palette
templateSystem.themes.cascade.1: Template override: add watermark + section spacing
templateSystem.themes.cascade.2: Variant tweak: en-business switches bilingual heading font
templateSystem.validation.title: Schema + example replay before render
templateSystem.validation.description: Render requests fail fast if the payload drifts from the JSON Schema. Named examples replay automatically in CI to protect variant versions before they hit production environments.

about.hero.title: Built to serve, not to maximize
about.hero.description: We're a small team that believes good software for municipalities shouldn't be held hostage by profit incentives.
about.section.0.title: A small team of founders running the company together
about.section.0.text.0: Epistola is owned and operated by about five people who co-founded the organization. We're not a venture-backed startup chasing growth at all costs—we're a group of founders building software we believe in, together.
about.section.0.text.1: We started Epistola because we saw municipalities struggling with document generation tools that were either expensive, inflexible, or locked them into vendor relationships. We wanted to create something better: reliable, open-source software that serves the public good.
about.section.0.text.2: Everyone here is a founder in spirit. We make decisions together, we're accountable to each other, and we move forward as a unified team.
about.section.1.title: Budget-driven pricing, not profit maximization
about.section.1.text.0: We set clear goals each year: what features we want to build, what infrastructure we need, what SLAs we commit to, and what team we can afford. We budget for those goals. Then we price the software to cover that budget—nothing more, nothing less.
about.section.1.text.1: Our revenue comes from three streams: feature development, SLA contracts, and donations. Not every organization needs or wants an SLA, but we still maintain the software, ship security patches, and keep things running. These donations—we call them levensvatbaarheidsbijdragen (viability contributions)—help keep the project sustainable for everyone.
about.section.1.text.2: If revenue grows beyond our budget, we have two choices: reinvest the surplus into the product and team, or reduce prices. Returns on investment are capped—we don't accumulate profit to maximize shareholder returns.
about.section.2.title: Formalizing our mission with a foundation
about.section.2.text.0: Today, our commitment to this mission rests on the values and promises of our founders. But we want to make it permanent. We're working toward creating a foundation (stichting) that will legally guard this mission for the long term.
about.section.2.text.1: The foundation will ensure that no future change of ownership or direction can override our core principles: fair pricing, open development, and service to the public good. Until then, we operate by the same principles and personally vouch for them.

privacy.title: How we handle data
privacy.intro: Epistola is an open-source renderer. When you self-host it, your organization remains in full control of all data. The following details cover how we treat information for our public website, demo environment, and optional managed service.
privacy.section.0.title: Website analytics
privacy.section.0.body: We only collect anonymous traffic statistics (page views, referrers, and device types) to understand how visitors discover Epistola. We do not use advertising cookies or share analytics data with third parties.
privacy.section.1.title: Demo environment
privacy.section.1.body: The hosted demo runs sample templates and placeholder data. Documents generated in the demo are automatically purged within 24 hours. Do not upload personal or production datasets to the demo instance.
privacy.section.2.title: Managed service
privacy.section.2.body: If you opt for Epistola's managed offering, a separate Data Processing Agreement (DPA) specifies responsibilities, retention, and regional hosting options. We only process the data required to provide the service and never reuse it for other purposes.
privacy.section.3.title: Contact
privacy.section.3.body: For privacy-related questions or DPA requests, email privacy@epistola.app.
security.title: Security practices
security.section.0.title: Code transparency
security.section.0.body: Epistola is open source. All changes are reviewed via pull requests and undergo automated linting, tests, and dependency scanning before release.
security.section.1.title: Self-hosting
security.section.1.body: When you run Epistola yourself, you control the infrastructure, networking, and access policies. We recommend isolating render nodes, enabling TLS everywhere, and monitoring outputs for unauthorized changes.
security.section.2.title: Managed service
security.section.2.body: Managed environments are deployed in dedicated namespaces with network policies, encrypted storage, and 24/7 monitoring. We support EU-region hosting on request.
security.section.3.title: Responsible disclosure
security.section.3.body: Report vulnerabilities privately to security@epistola.app. We'll acknowledge receipt within two business days and coordinate remediation steps.
terms.title: Terms of use
terms.section.0.title: Open-source usage
terms.section.0.body: Epistola's source code is licensed under the AGPL. You are free to self-host, modify, and redistribute the software under that license. Contributions are accepted via GitHub.
terms.section.1.title: Demo environment
terms.section.1.body: The public demo is provided "as is." We may reset, limit, or suspend access if the environment is misused. Do not store sensitive information in the demo instance.
terms.section.2.title: Commercial support
terms.section.2.body: Support and managed hosting plans are governed by separate service agreements. Those agreements outline SLAs, pricing, and acceptable use.
terms.section.3.title: Liability
terms.section.3.body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, Epistola and its contributors are not liable for any damages arising from the use of the open-source software, the demo environment, or third-party integrations.
terms.section.4.title: Contact
terms.section.4.body: For contractual questions, reach out to sander@epistola.app.
blog.title: Our Blog
blog.subtitle: Latest news, updates, and insights
